zh.lifeSummary: Margaret de Sacey是Osbern de Sacey Baron Clyst Gerred的女兒，於大約1077年出生在英國爾鄧翰 梅西。她於1098年與Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham在英國爾鄧翰 梅西結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1105年11月11日在英國英格蘭爾鄧翰 梅西享年29歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sacey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern, also found in south Wales): variant of   Veazey  .

variants.name: Veazey
meanings: English (Rutland): nickname from Anglo-Norman French enveisé ‘playful’ (Old French envoisié), Latinized as invesiatus (compare medieval Latin invasus ‘possessed by a demon’) and paralleled by lascivus ‘wanton’. The first syllable was lost early, resulting in numerous dialectal and orthographic variants; only Lenfestey preserves the fuller form, with an intrusive medial -t-. There has also been some confusion with Vessey .
History: John Veazey came from England to MD in the late 17th century. Thomas Ward Veazey (born 1774) was a MD legislator and planter.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Sacey was born about 1077, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England as the daughter of Osbern de Sacey Baron Clyst Gerred. She married Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham in 1098, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 11 November 1105, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret de Sacey est née environ 1077, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, fille de Osbern de Sacey Baron Clyst Gerred. Elle a épousé Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham en 1098, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 11 novembre 1105, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 29 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Sacey(은)는 대략 1077년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 Osbern de Sacey Baron Clyst Gerred의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1098년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1105년 11월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 던함 마시에서 29세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Sacey родилась около 1077 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия. Её отца звали Osbern de Sacey Baron Clyst Gerred. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hamon I de Massey 1st Baron of Dunham в 1098 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 11 ноября 1105 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 29 лет.
